CheckTitle: Ensure MFA is enabled for the root account
StatusExtended: MFA is not enabled for the root account.
Description: The root account is the most privileged user in an AWS account. MFA adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password. With MFA enabled, when a user signs in to an AWS Management Console, they will be prompted for their username and password as well as for an authentication code from their AWS MFA device.
Risk: If MFA is not enabled for the root account, an attacker who gains access to the root credentials could have full control over the entire AWS account.

Recommendation.Text: Enable MFA for the root account by going to IAM → Security credentials → Assign MFA device.

Requirements.Description: Ensure MFA is enabled for the root account
Virtual MFA should be enabled for the root user

CheckTitle: Ensure S3 buckets do not allow public access
StatusExtended: Bucket acme-data-archive has public read access enabled via bucket ACL.
Description: Amazon S3 buckets can be configured to allow public access. Allowing public access to S3 buckets can expose sensitive data to the internet.
Risk: Public read access to an S3 bucket can allow anyone on the internet to read or download the bucket's contents, potentially exposing sensitive or confidential data.

Code.Terraform: resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "example" { bucket = "acme-data-archive" block_public_acls = true block_public_policy = true ignore_public_acls = true restrict_public_buckets = true }

Recommendation.Text: Enable S3 Block Public Access settings on the bucket to prevent public access.

Requirements.Description: Ensure S3 bucket policy is set to deny HTTP requests

CheckTitle: Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
StatusExtended: No multi-region CloudTrail trail is enabled in account 123456789012.
Description: AWS CloudTrail is a web service that records AWS API calls for your account and delivers log files to you. The recorded information includes the identity of the API caller, the time of the API call, the source IP address of the API caller, the request parameters, and the response elements returned by the AWS service.
Risk: Without multi-region CloudTrail, API activity in some regions may go unlogged, reducing visibility into account activity and making incident response harder.

Recommendation.Text: Create a CloudTrail trail with multi-region logging enabled and store logs in a dedicated, access-controlled S3 bucket.

Requirements.Description: Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions

StatusExtended: EC2 instance i-0abc123def456789 does not require IMDSv2.
Description: EC2 Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) allows EC2 instances to access metadata about themselves. IMDSv2 is a more secure session-oriented method that requires a session token, preventing SSRF-based metadata theft.
Risk: Without IMDSv2, an SSRF vulnerability in an application running on EC2 could allow an attacker to steal IAM credentials from the metadata service.

Code.CLI: aws ec2 modify-instance-metadata-options --instance-id i-0abc123def456789 --http-tokens required --http-endpoint enabled

Recommendation.Text: Require IMDSv2 on all EC2 instances by setting HttpTokens to 'required' in instance metadata options.
